operationalSituations.description: An in-vehicle application uses the go-mqtt client (v3/v5) to publish and subscribe vehicle signals over MQTT in a production vehicle gateway — payloads carry signal values acted on by downstream control or display functions
The go-mqtt embedded broker brokers vehicle-signal traffic between in-vehicle ECUs and edge clients — every routing, retain, and QoS decision affects which subscriber receives which signal
The COVESA VISSR bridge maps VSS signal paths (dot notation) to MQTT topics (slash notation) and back — a mapping error misroutes a vehicle signal to the wrong topic space
CI runs relay conform --strict and the full go-FuSa lifecycle as a release gate before a go-mqtt version is tagged and consumed by covesa/vissr

hazards.description: Topic mismatch — MatchTopic (§4.7) routes a message to a subscriber whose filter should not match (or fails to deliver to one that should), so a signal reaches an unintended consumer or is silently lost
hazards.source: Incorrect single-level (+) or multi-level (#) wildcard handling, or off-by-one in topic-level segmentation, in MatchTopic

hazards.description: Envelope field loss — ToMessage / FromMessage drops or corrupts a mandatory field (topic, QoS, retained, payload), so a relay.Node routes a message that misrepresents the original vehicle signal
hazards.source: Missing field copy, incorrect Meta-map key, or faulty strconv conversion in ToMessage/FromMessage

hazards.description: QoS guarantee violated — a QoS 1 or QoS 2 publish is silently dropped, or a QoS 2 message is delivered more than once without deduplication, so a downstream function acts on a missing or duplicated signal
hazards.source: Incomplete PUBACK/PUBREC/PUBREL/PUBCOMP handshake, lost packet-identifier state, or back-pressure policy discarding an acknowledged message

hazards.description: Malformed wire packet — a CONNECT/PUBLISH/SUBSCRIBE control packet is encoded or decoded incorrectly, corrupting the byte stream so the peer mis-parses subsequent packets and acts on garbage signal data
hazards.source: Remaining-length encoding error, incorrect UTF-8 string length prefix, or fixed-header flag bug in v3/v5 packet encode/decode

hazards.description: Last-will not delivered — on an ungraceful client disconnect the broker fails to publish the registered will message, so downstream consumers never learn that a signal source has dropped offline
hazards.source: Will state not retained across the session, or will publish skipped on connection teardown

hazards.description: Resource exhaustion under fault — goroutine, connection, or subscription leakage under repeated connect/disconnect or fault injection grows unbounded, leading to eventual denial of signal transport
hazards.source: Subscription not closed on client Close, send-on-closed-channel guard missing, or context not cancelled on teardown

hazards.description: Concurrent-use corruption — simultaneous publishes or subscribe/close from multiple goroutines race on shared state and corrupt a payload or deliver a torn message
hazards.source: Unsynchronised access to subscription maps or buffers; a data race between deliver and close

safetyGoals.description: go-mqtt shall implement MatchTopic exactly per §4.7 such that a message is delivered to a subscription if and only if the topic matches the filter, for all single-level and multi-level wildcard cases
safetyGoals.safeState: Message delivered only to matching subscriptions; non-matching subscriptions receive nothing

safetyGoals.description: go-mqtt shall preserve every mandatory envelope field losslessly across ToMessage and FromMessage so the round-tripped relay.Message equals the canonical golden vector
safetyGoals.safeState: FromMessage(ToMessage(m)) reproduces topic, QoS, retained and payload exactly

safetyGoals.description: go-mqtt shall honour the requested QoS: a QoS 1 message is delivered at least once and a QoS 2 message exactly once, completing the full acknowledgement handshake before reporting success
safetyGoals.safeState: Publish returns success only after the QoS handshake completes; no acknowledged message is dropped or duplicated

safetyGoals.description: go-mqtt shall encode and decode every MQTT control packet strictly per the v3.1.1 / v5.0 wire format, rejecting malformed input with a sentinel error rather than producing or accepting a corrupt stream
safetyGoals.safeState: Malformed packets rejected with a sentinel error; the connection is not left in an ambiguous state

safetyGoals.description: The go-mqtt broker shall retain each client's registered last-will message and publish it on any ungraceful disconnect
safetyGoals.safeState: Will message published to subscribers on ungraceful disconnect

safetyGoals.description: go-mqtt shall release every goroutine, connection and subscription it owns when the owning client or broker is closed, so resource use stays bounded under repeated connect/disconnect and fault injection
safetyGoals.safeState: All owned resources released on Close; no leak across repeated lifecycles

safetyGoals.description: go-mqtt shall be safe for concurrent use by multiple goroutines such that concurrent publish, subscribe and close operations never race on shared state or corrupt a delivered message
safetyGoals.safeState: Concurrent operations are correctly synchronised; -race is clean and no message is torn
